public_reputation: Known around Ashforge Citadel through day-to-day work as shrine attendant.
manner: Protects small rituals with quiet firmness and notices subtle breaches long before they become public offense. In Ashforge Citadel, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of shrine attendant in and around Ashforge Citadel, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Ashforge Citadel workable through the obligations of shrine attendant while managing the immediate pressure of house rivalry, forge accidents, ashfall damage.
hidden_pressure: Must keep the role of shrine attendant credible while absorbing the strain created by house rivalry, forge accidents, ashfall damage.

adventure_hooks.summary: Edrin Chaincourt can draw the party into a locality problem centered on shrine attendant duties in Ashforge Citadel.
Edrin Chaincourt becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with house rivalry, forge accidents, ashfall damage around Ashforge Citadel.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Edrin Chaincourt is immediately readable as a shrine attendant tied to Ashforge Citadel.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Ashforge Citadel: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Edrin Chaincourt presents as someone shaped by the duties of a shrine attendant and the daily pressures of Ashforge Citadel.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a shrine attendant, and proof that strangers will not make Ashforge Citadel harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Edrin Chaincourt refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a shrine attendant too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can identify offerings, taboos, and recent signs of ritual irregularity.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Edrin Chaincourt adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Edrin Chaincourt always knows more about Ashforge Citadel than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a shrine attendant in Ashforge Citadel.
